topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp snap of the journal closing echoed in the otherwise silent dorm room. Maya froze, her hand hovering over the textbook she’d been trying to focus on. Her gaze flickered to Liam, who was now casually flipping through the pages, a faint, almost pleased smile playing on his lips. A cold knot tightened in Maya’s stomach, a heavy, sickening pressure that made her breath catch in her throat. She watched him, the casualness of his action feeling like a deliberate, invasive violation. Every nerve ending seemed to prickle with a sharp, unwelcome awareness. She wanted to bolt, to scream, but her feet felt cemented to the worn carpet. When he finally looked up, his eyes met hers, and for a split second, she saw something flicker—a fleeting acknowledgment, a shadow of something she couldn't quite decipher—before he returned to his reading, leaving Maya stranded in the sudden, suffocating silence.

[story 2]

I found the evidence tucked beneath my pillow: a page from my private diary, underlined in Liam’s distinctive blue pen. My hands were shaking so violently I had to press them flat against my desk, trying to steady the tremor that wracked my frame. It wasn't just the reading; it was the sheer intimacy of it, the way he’d absorbed my deepest anxieties and smallest triumphs. A flush crept up my neck, hot and undeniable, spreading across my cheeks. I kept glancing toward his side of the room, half-expecting him to look up, to acknowledge the silent accusation hanging in the air between us. When he finally glanced over, his expression was unreadable—a practiced neutrality that felt far more unsettling than any overt reaction. I quickly shoved the journal under my mattress, the movement jerky and clumsy, feeling the need to erase the very proof of my vulnerability.

[story 3]

The late afternoon light slanted through the window, illuminating the dust motes dancing above Liam’s desk. I heard the soft rustle of paper, a sound that sent a jolt of alarm straight through me. I crept closer, my heart hammering a frantic rhythm against my ribs. There, spread open on his textbook, was my entry from last Tuesday—a raw outpouring of fears I hadn't even known I'd written down. My vision blurred slightly. I felt a profound sense of exposure, as if every secret I’d ever held was now laid bare for his inspection. When he finally looked up, his expression was one of mild surprise, quickly masked by a shrug. "Just curious," he murmured, his tone light, utterly devoid of the weight I felt pressing down on my chest. The air in the room suddenly felt too thin to breathe.
